name: 5150 Martial Arts

contrastIssues: #3225BA on #F5F5F5: passes (8.1:1) — but the grey background mutes the electric indigo, making it read as template-flat rather than branded.
#188BF6 (secondary blue) clashes with #3225BA — two blue-family colors at different saturations fighting for the accent role.
colorAudit.harmonyType: monochromatic-indigo (after removing the competing secondary blue)
colorAudit.hierarchyStatus: weak — primary indigo and secondary blue were competing, and the unbranded greys gave the palette no anchor.
colorAudit.saturationNotes: #3225BA is a genuinely strong brand color (67% saturation, 44% lightness) — keep it. Drop the competing #188BF6. Anchor the palette on a rich obsidian dark-mode substrate so the indigo can read as an electric signal color.

colorShifts: Kept the exact brand indigo (#3225BA) as the primary accent — this IS 5150's signature color and it stays untouched.
Introduced #4D3ED9 as a brighter 'bright/glow' variant for hover states, call-to-action edges, and glow effects — lets the indigo feel electric rather than flat.
Dropped the secondary blue #188BF6 — it was fighting the primary indigo and adding no brand equity.
Replaced unbranded grey #F5F5F5 with a proper obsidian substrate (#0B0A14) tinted with the indigo hue — gives the palette emotional weight and lets the indigo signal hit harder.
Added #FFFFFF as a 'surface-white' for contrast sections — performance-athletic style alternates dark obsidian with stark white content blocks for maximum visual punch.

fontRecommendation.display: Space Grotesk 500-700 (headings) + Archivo Black (oversized hero display)
fontRecommendation.reasoning: Performance-athletic pairing. Space Grotesk brings geometric sportswear-brand energy for H1/H2. Archivo Black anchors the hero with a heavyweight display weight that echoes athletic-apparel graphic treatments. Inter handles body copy with clean, technical readability. All three are free via Google Fonts.
